revision.text:
[[ja:鮎貝八幡宮]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Ayukai Hachiman Shrine | image={{image requested|cat=Nishiokitama District|qq=}} | location=2303-2 Ayukai, Shirataka Town, Nishiokitama District, Yamagata Prefecture | coordinates={{Coord|38.190194444444444|N|140.07572222222223|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Ukanomitama|qid=Q3080728|1=Ukanomitama}}; {{ill|Ōjin|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} | founded=Kōhei 3 (1060) | type=Prefectural Shrine
Special Shrine | main hall=Three-bay Nagare-zukuri | festival=October 10 | Rituals=Temporary festival
Shichi-Go-San Lion Dance | map_type = Japan Yamagata Prefecture#Japan Dewa#Japan | prefecture={{ill|Shirataka|qid=Q1354787|1=Shirataka}} }} '''Ayukai Hachiman Shrine''' (鮎貝八幡宮, Ayukai Hachiman-gū) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Shirataka Town]], [[Nishiokitama District]], {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|en|Yamagata Prefecture|qid=Q125863|lt=Yamagata Prefecture|1=Yamagata Prefecture}}. The shrine's historical Rank is that of a Prefectural Shrine, and it is currently designated as a Special Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. The primary deity enshrined here is {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} ({{ill|Hachiman|en|八幡神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|qq=}}), with [[Ukanomitama]] ({{ill|Inari Ōkami|en|稲荷神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Ōkami|qq=}}) also enshrined. ==History== According to legend, the shrine was established in Kōhei 3 (1060) by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|en|Minamoto no Yoshiie|qid=Q32613|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie}}, who brought the Hachiman deity to this area and enshrined it, using the imperial flag as the sacred object. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, it served as a prayer site for the [[Uesugi clan]], the local feudal lords, and was designated as the main guardian shrine for 18 nearby villages. In Meiji 31 (1898), the shrine buildings were relocated to their current location on the grounds of the former Ayukai Castle. In Meiji 33 (1900), a nearby Inari shrine located in Hachimanmori was merged with the Hachiman Shrine. ==Cultural Properties== ===Yamagata Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties=== *main hall - Built in Tenpō 14 (1843). It was moved to its current location on the grounds of Ayukai Castle in Meiji 31. ===Shirataka Town Designated Intangible Cultural Properties=== *Shichi-Go-San Lion Dance ==festivals== During the annual grand festival on October 10, the Shichi-Go-San Lion Dance is performed. This dance, known for its unique footwork called "Seven Forward, Five Back, Three Turns," is believed to be the origin of the "Shichi-Go-San Dance" prevalent in the [[Okitama region]] and is designated as an intangible cultural property of Shirataka Town. {{Shinto Horizontal|qq=}} {{shinto-stub|qq=}} {{Beppyo Shrines|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|鮎貝八幡宮|version=101922593|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鮎貝八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11674156}} [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:山形県の旧県社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:白鷹町の建築物]] [[Category:白鷹町の歴史]] [[Category:西置賜郡の画像提供依頼]]
